purpose: Surface a variety of shoppable options as visual cards so the user can browse, compare, and pick one.
primaryUserGoal: Find an item that meets the user's requirements and budget, visually.
whenHermesShouldChoose: User asks for gift ideas, 'find me X', 'recommend me', 'show me options under $N', or general browsing with no single named product.
supports: Card image per product (via image.query for async resolution)
Optional action links to buy / read more
idealDataShape: Each with: title, price, 1-2 chips, 2-3 key bullets, image query

populationInstructions.summary: Produce tiled cards for each product candidate. Always include image.query so the card has a photo. Lead with 1-2 strong value chips (e.g. 'Top pick', 'Under $100'). Keep bullets to 2-3 concrete facts.
steps: Understand the user's constraints: budget, use case, preferred style.
Pick 4-12 products that match. Don't overload.
For each: set image.query to a specific photo search (brand + model works best).
Set price as a string with currency symbol.
Give each card 1-2 chips: 'Top pick', 'Budget', 'Premium', 'Under $X', etc.
Use bullets for concrete specs or differentiators, not marketing fluff.
guardrails: Keep card count under 12 — split into multiple recipes if needed.
Don't use bullet points for subjective claims ('great quality') — only concrete facts.

patchPrefer: Use upsert_cards to add new options as they are found
Use remove_items when the user rejects an option
